expectations.text: Asks at least 2 clarifying questions before proposing a solution
expectations.evidence: 5 questions asked (Q1-Q5): command type, commit range, changelog format, auto-write, grouping. Each with multiple-choice options and recommended defaults.

expectations.text: References actual files or patterns from the cape project codebase
expectations.evidence: References commands/brainstorm.md, CHANGELOG.md, hooks/session-start.sh, .claude-plugin/plugin.json. Notes 'Commit convention: type: description' from actual git history.

expectations.text: Proposes at least 2 distinct approaches with pros and cons
expectations.evidence: 3 approaches: (1) Claude Code slash command with inline instructions (recommended), (2) Shell script invoked by command, (3) Skill-based approach. Each has pros, cons, and reasoning.

expectations.text: Epic contains a Requirements (IMMUTABLE) section with specific, testable statements
expectations.evidence: Epic has '## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)' with 7 items, e.g., 'Command invoked as /cape:changelog generates a Keep a Changelog formatted section from git history' and 'Commits grouped by Conventional Commits type mapped to Keep a Changelog sections'.

expectations.text: Anti-patterns use 'NO X (reason: Y)' format with reasoning
expectations.evidence: 6 anti-patterns all with reasoning: 'NO auto-writing to CHANGELOG.md (reason: user must review and approve before any file modification)', 'NO shell script dependencies (reason: Cape is a pure markdown plugin...)', etc.

expectations.text: Creates exactly one first task, not zero or multiple
expectations.evidence: Exactly one task: 'Task 1: Create commands/changelog.md slash command' with goal, implementation steps, and success criteria.
No source code files created. Output is entirely design documentation — epic, task, and research findings.

expectations.text: Includes a Key Decisions table with Question/Answer/Implication columns
expectations.evidence: Key decisions table with 5 rows: Command type, Commit range, Changelog format, Auto-write, Grouping. All three columns present.
